text.title: MySQL + MongoDB Backup Implementation Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `MySQL + MongoDB Backup Implementation` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: mysqldump scripts MUST include `--single-transaction`, `--routines`, `--triggers`. Other flag combinations are reviewed before use.
rule.rationale: Omitting --single-transaction on busy InnoDB locks tables; omitting --routines silently drops stored procedures.

rule.statement: Hot physical backups on InnoDB MUST use Percona XtraBackup with `--prepare` + apply-log; raw file copy of InnoDB is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Raw copy of running InnoDB files is corrupt; XtraBackup uses InnoDB redo log for consistency.

rule.statement: MySQL PITR MUST preserve binary logs for ≥ the RPO target. Backups + binlog combination MUST be drilled end-to-end.
rule.rationale: Logical or physical backup alone does not give PITR; binlog is the delta layer.

rule.statement: Mongo backups MUST use mongodump from a secondary on a replica set; standalone-instance backup is forbidden for production.
rule.rationale: Standalone-instance backup has no consistent snapshot; replica-set secondary gives a drift-bounded snapshot.

rule.statement: Restore drills MUST run ≥ monthly for production; drill outcomes feed the RTO published in `backup-basics`.
rule.rationale: Untested DB backups are folklore; monthly drill keeps the RTO testable.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
